pattern: Try all pairs of elements to remove from the larger array, compute the implied shift, and return the minimum valid shift.
hints: Sort both arrays. After removing two elements from nums1, the remaining n-2 elements must match nums2 after adding x. The minimum of the remaining nums1 must map to min(nums2), so x = min(nums2) - (some element of nums1).

The two removed elements must come from the three candidates that are smallest in nums1 (since they bracket min(nums2) - x). Try removing each of the C(3,2)=3 pairs from sorted nums1 and check if the remainder plus x equals nums2.
For each candidate pair removal, compute x = sorted_nums2[0] - first_remaining and verify the full match with a two-pointer scan; track the minimum valid x.

edge_inputs.description: minimum size: nums1 length 3, nums2 length 1
positive shift with duplicates in nums1
multiple valid x values, must return minimum
removing first two elements of sorted nums1
large nums1 of length 5, shift by 0

stress.description: construct valid inputs by choosing x and two removal indices
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, min(size, 50))
    x = rng.randint(-200, 200)
    nums1 = [rng.randint(max(0, -x), min(1000, 1000 - x)) for _ in range(n)]
    # remove two indices
    rm = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), 2))
    nums2 = sorted([nums1[i] + x for i in range(n) if i not in rm])
    if not all(0 <= v <= 1000 for v in nums2):
        nums1 = list(range(3, n + 3))
        nums2 = sorted(nums1[2:])
        x = 0
    return [nums1, nums2]
